# Paris city guide

## Overview

Paris is the city that invented the art of doing nothing beautifully. Every corner has a cafe worth sitting in, every street has a building worth photographing, and the food scene ranges from perfect croissants to three-star temples of gastronomy. It's romantic, chaotic, and unapologetically itself.

**Best time to visit**: Spring (Apr-Jun) for mild weather and blooming gardens, Fall (Sep-Oct) for fewer crowds.
**Currency**: Euro (EUR). 1 EUR ≈ 1.08 USD.
**Language tip**: Always greet with "Bonjour" before anything else. It's the difference between rude and welcome.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Le Marais (3rd/4th) | Trendy, historic | Boutiques, galleries, falafel, LGBTQ+ scene | Metro Line 1, Saint-Paul |
| Montmartre (18th) | Bohemian, hilly | Sacre-Coeur, artists, wine bars | Metro Line 2, Anvers |
| Saint-Germain-des-Pres (6th) | Literary, chic | Cafes, bookshops, Luxembourg Gardens | Metro Line 4, Saint-Germain |
| Latin Quarter (5th) | Student, lively | Budget eats, Shakespeare & Co, Pantheon | Metro Line 10, Cluny-La Sorbonne |
| Champs-Elysees (8th) | Grand, touristy | Arc de Triomphe, luxury shopping | Metro Line 1, George V |
| Bastille (11th/12th) | Nightlife, local | Bars, markets, Rue de Lappe | Metro Line 1/5, Bastille |
| Belleville (20th) | Multicultural, artsy | Street art, Chinese food, panoramic views | Metro Line 2, Belleville |
| Canal Saint-Martin (10th) | Hip, relaxed | Brunch spots, vintage shops, canal walks | Metro Line 5, Jacques Bonsergent |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L'As du Fallafel | Street food | Le Marais | $ | The falafel that even non-falafel people obsess over. The line wraps around the block for a reason. | Special falafel pita with eggplant |
| Bouillon Chartier | French bistro | 9th | $ | A stunning 1896 dining hall serving classic French dishes at unbelievably low prices. Feel fancy without the bill. | Oeuf mayonnaise, steak frites, profiteroles |
| Du Pain et des Idees | Bakery | Canal Saint-Martin | $ | Possibly the best bakery in Paris. The pain des amis and escargot pastries are life-changing. | Escargot pistache-chocolat, pain des amis |
| Le Comptoir du Pantheon | Cafe | Latin Quarter | $$ | Classic Parisian cafe with a view of the Pantheon. Order a croque monsieur and watch the world go by. | Croque monsieur, cafe creme |
| Breizh Cafe | Creperie | Le Marais | $$ | Breton-style crepes with premium ingredients and proper cidre. The galette complete is perfect. | Galette complete (ham, egg, cheese), cidre |
| Chez Janou | French | Le Marais | $$ | Famous for the best chocolate mousse in Paris, served in a giant bowl you scoop yourself. | Chocolate mousse (unlimited), duck confit |
| Pink Mamma | Italian | 11th | $$ | Four floors of Italian food in a former warehouse. The truffle pizza and tiramisu are outstanding. | Truffle burrata pizza, tiramisu |
| Le Bouillon Pigalle | French bistro | Pigalle | $ | Modern bouillon with beautiful decor and old-school French dishes. Great value. | Soupe a l'oignon, blanquette de veau |
| Cafe de Flore | Cafe | Saint-Germain | $$$ | Where Sartre and de Beauvoir held court. Overpriced but iconic. Get a hot chocolate on the terrace. | Chocolat chaud, croissant |
| Septime | Fine dining | 11th | $$$ | One of the best modern French tasting menus in Paris. Book weeks in advance. The wine pairings are exceptional. | Seasonal tasting menu |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Eiffel Tower (Tour Eiffel) | Iconic | 7th | $$ | 2-3 hours | Skip the summit line by booking online. The second floor has the best photo angles. Sparkles every hour after dark. |
| Louvre Museum (Musee du Louvre) | Museum | 1st | $$ | 3-4 hours | You cannot see it all. Pick a wing, see the Mona Lisa, then get lost. Friday evenings are less crowded. |
| Sacre-Coeur Basilica | Culture | Montmartre | Free | 1-2 hours | Climb the hill for the best free view in Paris. The basilica is stunning inside. Street performers outside. |
| Musee d'Orsay | Museum | 7th | $$ | 2-3 hours | Impressionist masterpieces in a converted train station. The clock windows are worth the visit alone. |
| Luxembourg Gardens (Jardin du Luxembourg) | Nature | 6th | Free | 1-2 hours | Paris's most beautiful park. Bring a book, rent a model sailboat, watch Parisians being Parisian. |
| Sainte-Chapelle | Culture | Ile de la Cite | $$ | 1 hour | Gothic chapel with the most spectacular stained glass windows in the world. Go on a sunny day. |
| Pere Lachaise Cemetery | Culture | 20th | Free | 2 hours | Oscar Wilde, Jim Morrison, Chopin, Edith Piaf. A beautiful, sprawling cemetery that feels like an outdoor museum. |
| Montmartre Village Walk | Walk | 18th | Free | 2-3 hours | Wander from Place du Tertre through cobblestone streets past vineyard and windmills. Feels like a village. |
| Centre Pompidou | Art | Le Marais | $$ | 2-3 hours | Inside-out architecture housing modern art. The rooftop terrace has panoramic views of Paris. |
| Seine River Cruise | Scenic | Central | $$ | 1 hour | Take a Bateaux Mouches at sunset. Tourist classic but genuinely beautiful when the bridges light up. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rue de Lappe | Bars | Bastille | $-$$ | A street packed with bars, from salsa clubs to dive bars. Bastille's nightlife hub. |
| Le Perchoir Marais | Rooftop bar | Le Marais | $$ | Rooftop cocktails with views over Parisian rooftops. Arrive early for a good spot. |
| Concrete | Club | 13th | $$ | Floating nightclub on a barge by the Seine. Techno, house, open-air terrace. |
| Harry's New York Bar | Cocktail bar | 2nd | $$$ | Hemingway drank here. Birthplace of the Bloody Mary. Old-school, wood-paneled, perfect. |
| Le Caveau de la Huchette | Jazz club | Latin Quarter | $$ | Jazz and swing in a medieval cellar since 1946. Dancing every night. |

## Transit tips

- **Metro**: 16 lines, runs 5:30 AM to 1:15 AM (2:15 AM Fri-Sat). Covers the entire city. Buy a carnet of 10 tickets for savings.
- **Navigo Easy card**: Load it at any station. Tap on/off for Metro, bus, and RER.
- **RER**: Regional trains for Versailles (Line C), CDG Airport (Line B), and Disneyland (Line A).
- **Walking**: Paris is compact. You can walk from the Eiffel Tower to the Marais in about 45 minutes.
- **Velib bikes**: Cheap bike-share stations everywhere. Great for Canal Saint-Martin and along the Seine.
- **Pro tip**: Avoid the Metro during rush hour (8-9:30 AM, 5:30-7:30 PM). Line 13 is always packed.
- **Airport**: CDG is far. Allow 1.5 hours by RER B. Orly is closer, use OrlyBus or tram.

## Useful French phrases

| English | French | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Bonjour | bohn-ZHOOR |
| Thank you | Merci | mehr-SEE |
| Please | S'il vous plait | seel voo PLEH |
| The bill please | L'addition, s'il vous plait | lah-dee-SYOHN seel voo PLEH |
| Excuse me | Excusez-moi | ex-koo-ZAY mwah |
| Do you speak English? | Parlez-vous anglais? | par-LAY voo ahn-GLEH |
| Delicious! | Delicieux! | day-lee-SYUH |
| Cheers! | Sante! | sahn-TAY |
